mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value < 0.05, or rolling Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals labor market deterioration that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that boosts corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative median 60-day returns, or the interaction term loses predictive power with p-value > 0.10 in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in > 70% of triggered observations, or the signal reverses direction during easing periods without statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising core inflation and stagnant federal funds rates creates real rate compression that fuels equity risk appetite, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.4% while FEDFUNDS change < 0.1%
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when real rates compress, or the interaction effect vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.8% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports wage-driven spending, mechanically lifting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with payroll growth with Rank IC < 0.02, or the signal breaks down during periods of productivity stagnation.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers recession risk pricing that compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of GDP contraction quarters, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in industrial production and housing starts signals broad-based economic slowdown that accelerates short-term capital flight from equities, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive when both series decline simultaneously, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-series benchmark in out-of-sample tests.
